message:
perf(mvcc): gate active-snapshots gauge off txn begin/release hot path

Extend the `MVCC_SNAPSHOT_METRICS_ENABLED` gate (landed in bc4fa6b5)
to cover the two sibling gauge updates that were still unconditional:

- `mvcc_snapshot_established()`: one relaxed `AtomicU64::fetch_add`,
  called from every `TransactionManager::begin` that establishes a
  read snapshot (lifecycle.rs:717 / 782 / 1284). These run on every
  BEGIN CONCURRENT, every implicit-read transaction, and every
  explicit read-only session.
- `mvcc_snapshot_released()`: a compare-and-swap loop on the same
  `MVCC_ACTIVE_SNAPSHOTS` counter, called from the transaction
  finalization path (lifecycle.rs:1740).

Both were under the same "snapshot-read metrics" umbrella as the
histogram I gated in bc4fa6b5 — they're surfaced via the same
`SnapshotReadMetricsSnapshot` reader (`fsqlite_mvcc_active_snapshots`
field) and have no production consumer outside one diagnostic test.
Reuse `MVCC_SNAPSHOT_METRICS_ENABLED` so one flag controls the whole
family. `mvcc_snapshot_established` is now `#[inline]` with an
early-return on one relaxed bool load; the full CAS loop moves into
`#[cold] #[inline(never)] mvcc_snapshot_released_slow`.

Also fix a bc4fa6b5 regression: `lifecycle::tests::test_snapshot_read_span_and_metrics`
asserts on the gauge + histogram, but bc4fa6b5 forgot to enable the
gate in this test, so it was failing. Flip the gate on at the top.

Before: 4.04 ns per (established + released) cycle
After:  0.77 ns per (established + released) cycle
Delta:  -81.1%, all 9 trials 4.04→0.77 (no variance between samples)
Bench:  bench_snapshot_established_released_gate (added here; tight
        est+rel loop, 4M cycles/trial, n=9)
Cmd:    cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --release \
        bench_snapshot_established_released_gate -- --ignored --nocapture

Correctness: `test_snapshot_read_span_and_metrics` now passes (was
failing pre-commit),
`snapshot_metrics_record_histogram_and_gauge` still passes (already
flipped the gate since bc4fa6b5), `cas_metrics_recording_buckets_progress`
still passes.

Bead: bd-1j7vk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
